programs.excerpt: Need one source of truth for consent, suppression, and attribution posture before the reactivation push.
programs.nextAction: Restore consent evidence and close the stale suppression packet.

programs.audience: US active subscribers by replenishment cohort
programs.excerpt: Deliverability and suppression checkpoints are healthy, but the final attribution packet should still be archived.
programs.nextAction: Confirm the attribution archive and keep the replenishment window green.

programs.audience: High-LTV subscribers in the priority cohort
programs.excerpt: Suppression logic, approval posture, and deliverability guardrails are still split across marketing and RevOps teams.
programs.nextAction: Repair the delivery posture before the VIP promotion goes live.

packets.message: Consent evidence still does not reconcile historical opt-in posture with the reactivation audience export.
The flow still lacks one consolidated suppression artifact for bounced, unsubscribed, and complaint-risk recipients.

packets.audience: US active subscribers by replenishment cohort
packets.message: Attribution packet approved and archived against the replenishment cohort.

packets.audience: High-LTV subscribers in the priority cohort
packets.message: Deliverability safeguards are incomplete for the VIP launch; the send could ship before the latest domain warmup and throttling evidence are locked.

packets.audience: High-LTV subscribers in the priority cohort
packets.message: The VIP campaign still lacks one owner-safe attribution chain for experiment holdout logic and downstream revenue proof.
